Japanese: もちろん‥‥犯人が凶器として 用意していた、小刀です。
English: Naturally, the dagger the killer brought to the scene of the crime.

Japanese: あ‥‥そういえば！ 忘れていましたな‥‥
English: Oh...! That's right! I forgot about that...

Japanese: この小刀は、現場にあった 松の木に刺さっていたそうですね。
English: This dagger was found at the crime scene, stuck into a pine tree.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。今朝、刑事が見つけて オレのところへ持ってきた。
English: Yes, the detective found that this morning and brought it to me.

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》は、これで 犯人に反撃したのです！
English: Dahlia Hawthorne struck back at the killer with this!

Japanese: そして‥‥犯人の身体に、 《傷》を負わせた！
English: And she managed to wound him as well!

Japanese: 悲鳴を上げたからといって、 傷を負ったとはかぎらねえ。
English: Just because he let out a scream doesn't mean that he was wounded.

Japanese: 小刀に付着した血痕は、 被害者のものかもしれねえぜ！
English: For all we know, the blood on the dagger could have been from the victim!

Japanese: すでに、血痕の検査が済んで いるのを、お忘れですか？
English: Have you forgotten that the blood has already been tested?

Japanese: 被害者のものでない以上‥‥ これは、犯人の血痕なのです！
English: Since we learned it wasn't the victim's blood, it must be the killer's blood!

Japanese: 犯人が、身体のどこかを 負傷したのは、まちがいない！
English: The killer must have a wound somewhere on his body!

Japanese: その小刀に残った血痕が‥‥ 《犯人》の血だ、と‥‥？
English: So you're saying the blood on this dagger belongs to the killer...?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 血痕の遺伝子情報を検査すれば、 カンペキに立証できるはずです！
English: A DNA analysis of the blood would prove it beyond a shadow of a doubt!

Japanese: ‥‥そう！　これは、 あなたの血であると！
English: And yes, Mr. Godot. It would prove that it's your blood!

Japanese: おもしれェじゃねえか。
English: Nice theory, Trite.

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: （‥‥これで、キマリだ‥‥）
English: (Is this the end...? Have I done it...?)

Japanese: （いくらアイツでも、科学調査の 結果を変えることは、不可能！）
English: (Even he won't be able to change the results of a scientific test!)

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！
なあ‥‥まるほどう。
English: Let me ask you something, Trite.

Japanese: 仮に、オレが犯人だと 考えてみようか。
English: Let's just say that it turned out that I was the killer.

Japanese: このオレが‥‥そんな マヌケな証拠を残すと思うかい？
English: Do you really think I would be stupid enough to leave evidence like that?

Japanese: なんだって‥‥
English: What...?

Japanese: いいか。よく思い出してみな。
English: Just think for a second.

Japanese: その小刀は、今朝。刑事が見つけて オレのところへ持ってきた。
English: This dagger was found this morning by a detective and brought to me.

Japanese: その時点で、たしかに 血痕は付着していたさ。
English: There was already a bloodstain on it, correct?

Japanese: だが‥‥‥
English: But even so...

Japanese: コイツを刑事から受け取って ここへ持って来たのは、オレだぜ。
English: I was the one who brought this dagger here to the courtroom.

Japanese: そ‥‥それが、いったい‥‥？
English: Y-Yes... What does that prove?

Japanese: あくまでも、仮に‥‥ オレが犯人ならば。
English: Well, if I really were the killer...

Japanese: 小刀を完全に洗浄して、ベツの 血痕をデッチ上げただろうぜ。
English: ...I could've washed the blade off and then planted another person's blood on it.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥バカな‥‥！
English: Th-That's... It can't be...!

Japanese: とにかく、これだけは 保証してやるさ、まるほどう。
English: In any case, there's one thing I can guarantee, Trite.

Japanese: その血痕‥‥ オレの血とは、一致しねえさ。
English: That blood... It doesn't belong to me.

Japanese: ‥‥ゼッタイにな！
English: Not a chance!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですってェェッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥犯人が負傷したのは ジジツのようですね。
English: In any event, it seems to be established that the killer was wounded.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてくださ
English: Alright then, witness. Continue your testimony.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと‥‥ 待ってくださいっ！
English: W-Wait a minute...!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですかな？　証人。
English: ...What's the problem?

Japanese: あ、あたしがこんなコト 言うのもアレなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, I... I know I probably shouldn't say this, but...

Japanese: なるほどくんのセツメイには‥‥ 大きなムジュンがありますっ！
English: ...there's a big contradiction in Nick's explanation!

Japanese: ま‥‥‥真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: その、小刀‥‥犯人を傷つけた、 って言ってたよね。
English: Th-This dagger... You said that it wounded the killer.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだよ。
English: ...That's right.

Japanese: でも‥‥‥ でも、でも‥‥！
English: But... But, but...!

Japanese: ‥‥もし、ゴドー検事が カタナで傷つけられたのなら‥‥
English: If Mr. Godot had really been cut with the dagger...

Japanese: ‥‥服に血がついたり、 破れているハズじゃないですか！
English: ...his clothes should be bloody or have a rip in them, right!?

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ、真宵ちゃん。
English: ...Umm, Maya?

Japanese: そんなの、着替えれば 済むハナシじゃないか！
English: Maybe he just changed his clothes? That'd solve the contradiction pretty easily.

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの！ そういうコトじゃないよ！
English: What are you talking about? It's not that simple at all!

Japanese: 事件があった日のこと、 思い出してみて！
English: Remember back to the day of the murder!

Japanese: 《奥の院》側にいたヒトは‥‥ 閉じこめられていたんだよ！
English: Everyone that was on the Inner Temple side got trapped there!

Japanese: あッ！　そ‥‥そうです！
English: Ah! Th-That's right!

Japanese: そして‥‥橋が修理されて、警察が 《奥の院》に向かったとき‥‥
English: And once the bridge was fixed and the police headed for the Inner Temple...

Japanese: そこには、ゴドー検事も いたはずでしょ！
English: ...Mr. Godot was already there waiting for them!

Japanese: 着替える服も、ヒマも なかったじゃない！
English: He never had a chance to change his clothes!

Japanese: ぎゃあああああッ！
English: Aaaaack!

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ たしかに、それはなんと
English: Order! Order in the court! What the witness says is true--

Japanese: あ‥‥あらかじめ、着替えを 用意しておいたかもしれない！
English: W-Well, maybe he brought a change of clothes with him!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: 落雷によって橋が焼失したのは、 あくまでもグーゼンですぞ。
English: No one could have predicted the lightning strike that shut down the bridge!

Japanese: 着替えなど、用意するハズが ないではないですかッ！
English: Why would anyone have brought a change of clothes!?

Japanese: うう‥‥ッ！ （くそ。裁判長のクセに‥‥）
English: Nngh...! (Did the judge take smart pills during the last recess?)

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ！　‥‥犯行の際、 犯人は服を脱いだのかもしれない！
English: Well then! Maybe the killer took off his clothes before he committed the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥返り血が付着するのを恐れて！
English: That way he wouldn't get any blood on them!

Japanese: あり得ねえよ、まるほどう。
English: That's impossible, Trite.

Japanese: 夜の吾童山のサムさは‥‥ アンタもよく、知ってるハズだ。
English: You know how cold it gets up there late at night!
Eh...

Japanese: 何も着てなかったら‥‥ すぐに、動けなくなっちゃうぜ！
English: After a few minutes with no clothes on, you'd be frozen solid!

Japanese: うおおおおおおお‥‥ッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ふ、ふむむむううう‥‥
English: Hmm... Hmmm... Hmmmm...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
やっぱり‥‥ このテイドなんだよ、アンタは。
English: So that's all you've got... I knew you weren't tough enough to finish this.
Urk...

Japanese: こんなとき‥‥ もし、綾里　千尋なら。
English: Right now... if Mia Fey were here...

Japanese: 綾里　千尋なら‥‥‥ たった一度でキメただろうぜ。
English: If Mia Fey were here... She would have closed the book on this case already.

Japanese: （“千尋さんなら”‥‥？）
English: (If Mia Fey were here...?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうだい、まるほどう！ アンタには、できるのか？
English: So come on, Trite! Can you do it or not!?

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、弁護人。
English: How about it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あなたは、ゴドー検事を 《犯人》として告発しています。
English: You've accused Mr. Godot of being the killer.

Japanese: それを‥‥立証できますか？ たった１つの証拠品で！
English: But can you prove it? Have you got even one piece of evidence!?

Japanese: （“立証できるか”‥‥？ そんなことは、問題じゃない）
English: (The question isn't whether I can prove it or not...)

Japanese: （“立証する” ‥‥ぼくには、それしかない）
English: (The fact is, I have to prove it! That's the only choice I have.)

Japanese: （‥‥それが、ぼくが学んだ 《弁護士》のルールなんだ！）
English: (I was taught that it's one of the rules of being a lawyer!)

Japanese: ‥‥立証します。
English: ...I can prove it.

Japanese: ‥‥お望みどおり、華麗に 引導をたたきつけましょう！
English: I'm going to bring your magnificent vengeance to fruition, just as you want it.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ かかったな、まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! That's good... A fighter till the bitter end, Trite...

Japanese: 証拠品が、１つ。 それならば‥‥
English: Since there's just one piece of evidence that can prove your point...

Japanese: 無限大のペナルティを 背負ってもらおうか？
English: ...why don't we go for the unlimited penalty?

Japanese: プレッシャーをかけるつもり ですか？　ゴドー検事‥‥
English: Are you trying to pressure me, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ‥‥かまいませんよ。 証拠品は、１つきりですからね。
English: Because it doesn't matter to me. I've got the one piece of evidence I need.

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！　アンタなんかに できるわけがねえ！
English: G-Give me a break! You've got nothing, Trite!

Japanese: （こんなとき、ぼくなら どうするか‥‥？）
English: (So what do I do at a time like this...?)

Japanese: （‥‥決まってるじゃないか！ 発想を《逆転》させるんだ！）
English: (It's simple! I've gotta think outside the box and approach this from a different angle!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 聞かせていただきましょうか。
English: Alright then, Mr. Wright. Let's hear what you've got.

Japanese: ‥‥さきほどの尋問で、１つ。 立証されたことがあります。
English: ...There's one thing I've demonstrated in the previous cross-examination.

Japanese: 《犯人が、傷を受けたこと》‥‥。 小刀の血痕が、その証拠です。
English: The killer was wounded. That was proven by the blood on the dagger.

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥！
English: B-But...

Japanese: その傷をかくすことは 《不可能》だったのでは！
English: We decided it was impossible for him to have hidden such a wound!

Japanese: カタナで切られたのならば、 衣服に痕跡が残ったハズです！
English: If he had been cut by a dagger, there should have been a bloodstain on his clothing.

Japanese: ‥‥１カ所だけ‥‥
English: There's one place...

Japanese: 犯人には、傷をかくせる 場所があったのです。
English: One place the killer could have hidden his wound.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: Wh-What did you say!?

Japanese: き、傷を‥‥かくした‥‥
English: H-hidden...?

Japanese: （この、最後の瞬間‥‥今こそ、 発想を《逆転》させろ！）
English: (This is it! My last stand! I need to think about this from a different angle!)

Japanese: （服に痕跡が残らなかった《理由》 を考えるのではなくて‥‥）
English: (I don't need to think about why there were no bloodstains on his clothing...)

Japanese: （犯人が、傷をかくした‥‥ その《方法》をたたきつける！）
English: (I need to show how he hid the wound!)

Japanese: これが、最後だな‥‥まるほどう！ 聞かせてもらおうか。
English: It's the end of the line! The final stop, Trite! Let's hear what you've got!

Japanese: 犯人が傷を《かくした場所》‥‥ いったい、どこだッ！
English: Where's this location where you say the killer hid his wound!?

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！　ナニを 投げつけたか知らねえが‥‥
English: Ha...! I don't know what you're talking about...

Japanese: わざわざ、見るまでもねえさ。
English: And frankly, I don't need to know.

Japanese: アンタなんぞに、アイツを超える 弁護なんざ、できるワケがねえ！
English: What I do know is that you'll never be half the lawyer she was!

Japanese: そうだろう？　まるほどう‥‥
English: Isn't that right, Trite...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 立証されたようですな。
English: Well, it looks like you've proven one thing.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥ “ニセモノ”である、と！
English: That you're nothing but a fake!

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってください！ もう一度、チャンスを
English: W-Wait a minute! Give me one more chance!

Japanese: それでは！　ヤクソクを 守っていただきましょう。
English: I don't think so! A promise is a promise!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　楽しかったぜ。
English: Ha...! It was fun while it lasted.

Japanese: ‥‥あばよ！　まるほどう‥‥
English: See you later... Trite...

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！　ナニを 投げつけたか知らねえが‥‥
English: ...Ha! I don't know what you're talking about...

Japanese: わざわざ、見るまでもねえさ。
English: And frankly, I don't need to know.

Japanese: アンタなんぞに、アイツを超える 弁護なんざ、できるワケがねえ！
English: What I do know is that you'll never be half the lawyer she was!

Japanese: そうだろう？　まるほどう‥‥
English: Isn't that right, Trite...?

Japanese: （な、なんだ‥‥今のは‥‥）
English: (Wh-What was that just now?)
...Mia?

Japanese: （う‥‥ウソだッ！）
English: (It-It can't be!)

Japanese: （オマエが‥‥アイツの中に 生きている、なんて‥‥）
English: (You're living on... through him...!?)

Japanese: あなたは‥‥ 今も《傷》をかくしているのです！
English: Even as we speak, you're still hiding the wound!

Japanese: その、マスクの下に！
English: It's beneath your mask!

Japanese: 犯人が発していた《赤い光》は 格闘中、突然消えました。
English: During the fight, the red lights given off by the killer suddenly disappeared.

Japanese: そして‥‥次の瞬間、 悲鳴が上がった‥‥
English: Seconds later, the killer let out a scream.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。あなたのマスクは、 ふっ飛ばされたのです！
English: That's right. Your mask went flying off your face!

Japanese: ゴドー検事。‥‥マスクを はずしていただけますか。
English: Mr. Godot! Would you mind removing your mask?

Japanese: そこに、小刀による《傷》を カクニンできれば‥‥
English: If you have a dagger wound under there somewhere...

Japanese: ‥‥それで、この事件は 完全に解決します！
English: ...then I'd say this whole case is solved!

Japanese: ぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAARGH!!
Just now...

Japanese: アンタに、アイツの姿が 重なって見えたよ。
English: I saw her spirit in you.

Japanese: アンタが、気に入らなかった。
English: I never liked you.

Japanese: ‥‥今から、６年前‥‥
English: Six years ago...

Japanese: アンタは‥‥オレを“眠らせた” 女に協力して、《毒》をかくした。
English: ...you helped the woman who put me to sleep by hiding her bottle of poison.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And then...

Japanese: オレが眠っているあいだに、 彼女を‥‥死なせた。
English: While I was sleeping... ...you let Mia die.

Japanese: そのくせ、テメエ自身は 平気なカオで生きながらえて‥‥
English: But you didn't care. You just kept living your pathetic, happy-go-lucky life.

Japanese: あろうことか、彼女の後継者を キドっていやがる。
English: You even had the nerve to follow in her footsteps as a lawyer.

Japanese: そんなアンタを、ユルせねえ。 ‥‥そう思っていた。
English: I could never forgive you. That's what I thought.

Japanese: 検事さん‥‥‥
English: Mr. Godot...

Japanese: だが‥‥そうじゃなかった。 最初から、わかっていたのさ。
English: But... I was wrong about you. I knew it from the very beginning.

Japanese: ‥‥なんのコトはねえ。オレが 本当にユルせなかったのは‥‥
English: The truth is, the only person I could never find it in my heart to forgive...

Japanese: この、オレ自身だったんだ。
English: ...was me.

Japanese: あなた‥‥自身‥‥
English: Y-You, yourself...?

Japanese: チヒロを守れなかったのは、 他のダレでもねえ。
English: I was the one that failed to protect Mia...

Japanese: ‥‥このオレだ。
English: ...Me and no one else.

Japanese: そのジジツから目をそらして‥‥ オレは、逃げ出しちまった。
English: I tried to avert my eyes from the truth... to escape from the harshness of reality.

Japanese: チヒロの死に、真正面から 向かい合うことから、逃げた！
English: I just couldn't face Mia's death head on, so I ran!

Japanese: ‥‥冷たい仮面でカオをかくして、 本当の名前まで、ほうり投げて‥‥
English: I hid behind a mask. I threw away my true name.

Japanese: 弁護士であることすら、 やめちまった。
English: I couldn't even deal with being a defense attorney anymore, so I quit.

Japanese: でも‥‥！　あなたは、 真宵ちゃんを救ってくれました！
English: But, but! You saved Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 オレも、そのつもりだったさ。
English: Yeah. That was my plan...

Japanese: ついさっきまでは、な。
English: Up until just now, anyway.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: いいか、おじょうちゃん。
English: Are you listening, Maya?

Japanese: もし‥‥オレが純粋に、アンタを 助けようと思っていたのならば‥‥
English: If I had really wanted to save you...

Japanese: まっ先に、相談するべき 人物がいたんじゃねえか‥‥？
English: ...then there's one person that I should have gone and talked to right away.

Japanese: ‥‥それ、って‥‥
English: Wh-Who would that be...?

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥ですか？
English: Are you talking about Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥だが、オレはそうしなかった。
English: But I didn't do it...

Japanese: あやめさんと、アンタのおかあさん には、協力を求めたのに‥‥
English: I tried to get the help of Iris and your mother...

Japanese: イチバン重要な男には、 あえて目をつぶった。
English: But I closed my eyes to the most important man involved.

Japanese: わかるかい？　‥‥その理由が。
English: Do you know why? The real reason?

Japanese: どうして、ですか‥‥？
English: No... Why?

Japanese: オレは‥‥たぶん、アンタを 救おうとしたんじゃねえのさ。
English: I suppose... I wasn't really interested in saving you at all.

Japanese: ただ‥‥カラッポな、自分の ココロを救いたかっただけ‥‥
English: I think I was just trying to salvage what's left of my own broken soul...

Japanese: チヒロを守れなかった自分に、 《言いワケ》したかったのさ！
English: I was trying to make up for the fact that I couldn't save Mia. Nothing more.

Japanese: だから‥‥アンタを、あんなに あぶない目にあわせちまった。
English: That's why I let you walk right into a situation that I knew was dangerous.

Japanese: ‥‥すまなかった。
English: Forgive me...

Japanese: ち‥‥ちがう！
English: Y-You're wrong!

Japanese: あなたは、真宵ちゃんのために‥‥ 命をかけて、戦ってくれたんです！
English: You put your life on the line to save Maya!

Japanese: どうだったんだろうな‥‥？ 自分でも、よくわからねえんだ。
English: Was it really for Maya's sake...? Even I'm not really sure.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that...?

Japanese: ‥‥あの夜。中庭の闇の中に アイツの影が見えたとき‥‥
English: That night, in the darkness of the garden, when I saw her silhouette...

Japanese: ‥‥ココロの片スミで、オレには わかっていたハズなんだ。
English: A part of me must have known the truth...

Japanese: 『コイツは《美柳　ちなみ》 じゃねえ』‥‥ってな。
English: The truth that it wasn't really Dahlia Hawthorne standing there in front of me.

Japanese: 綾里　舞子か‥‥もしかしたら、 あの少女だったかもしれねえ。
English: It could have been Misty Fey... Or even that little girl.

Japanese: だが‥‥オレは、ムチュウで 仕込みヅエをふり上げた！
English: But I still picked up the blade... It was like I was dreaming!

Japanese: あの一瞬、オレのココロに‥‥ 本当は、何があった‥‥？
English: I'm not sure exactly what was going on in my mind at that point...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵を守ろうとする、 純粋なキモチだったのか？
English: Was I really motivated by the pure desire to protect Maya Fey?

Japanese: それとも‥‥
English: Or was it something else...?

Japanese: ６年前。オレのすべてを 奪った、あのオンナに対する‥‥
English: Was it my hatred for a woman who had stolen everything from me 6 years earlier...?

Japanese: 決してむくわれることのない 《復讐》だったのか‥‥？
English: Could it have been simply a desire for revenge?

Japanese: 今となっては‥‥ もう、わからねえのさ！
English: And now, I don't know anything anymore!

Japanese: だが‥‥今日。 やっと、わかったことがある。
English: I did learn something today, however.

Japanese: 思い上がってたのは‥‥ オレのほうだった、ってな。
English: I finally realized that I was the arrogant one.

Japanese: オレは‥‥ただ、 マボロシを追っていたんだ。
English: I was just... chasing an illusion, a fantasy.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに勝つという、 ありもしないマボロシを‥‥
English: The stupid fantasy of defeating you in the courtroom...

Japanese: 教えてくれたのは、アンタさ。
English: You were the one who made me realize my folly.

Japanese: アンタは、逃げなかった。 彼女の‥‥チヒロの死から。
English: You never ran away from Mia's death.

Japanese: そして‥‥彼女の《弁護》を、 シッカリ受け継いでいた。
English: Instead, you picked up where she left off, as a true defender of the people.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、一瞬‥‥。 それが、ハッキリわかったのさ！
English: In that one moment... I understood everything!

Japanese: ゴドー‥‥検事‥‥
English: Mr. Godot...

Japanese: よかったら‥‥ おぼえておいてくれるかい？
English: I think you already know this, but if you don't...

Japanese: オレの名前は‥‥ 神乃木　荘龍、だぜ。
English: My name is... Diego Armando.

Japanese: か‥‥神乃木さん！
English: M-Mr. Armando!

Japanese: あたし‥‥あたしは、 信じてますから。
English: I believe in you!

Japanese: あなたは‥‥ あたしを助けてくれたんだ、って！
English: I know you were trying to save me!

Japanese: ‥‥‥ああ。ありがとうよ。
English: Hmm... Thanks.

Japanese: き‥‥傷口から‥‥血が！
English: Y-Your wound...! It's bleeding!

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！　忘れたのかい。
English: ...Ha! Did you forget already?

Japanese: ‥‥オレの世界に、 《赤》は存在しねえんだぜ。
English: In my world, the color red doesn't exist.

Japanese: こいつは、きっと‥‥ オレの《涙》なのさ。
English: These must be... my tears.

Japanese: ‥‥なみだ‥‥
English: Tears...?

Japanese: 長い眠りからさめた、 あの日から‥‥
English: Ever since I woke up from my coma...

Japanese: ‥‥オレはずっと、このときを 待っていたのかもしれねえ。
English: ...I think I've been waiting for this very moment.

Japanese: か‥‥神乃木さん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Armando...

Japanese: ‥‥おぼえておくといい、 おじょうちゃん。
English: You'd do well to remember this, Maya.

Japanese: オトコが泣くのは‥‥ すべてを終えたときだけ、だぜ。
English: The only time a lawyer can cry is when it's all over.

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥ すべてが終わったのですね。
English: This time... It really is all over, isn't it.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。
English: ...Defendant.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたは、殺人には直接 カンケイしていませんが‥‥
English: Although you weren't directly involved in the murder...

Japanese: 遺体の移動や現場の工作は、 リッパな罪になります。
English: ...tampering with the body and the crime scene is a serious offense in itself.

Japanese: もちろん、わかっています。
English: I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥神乃木さまから、すでに 何度もうかがっておりますから‥‥
English: Mr. Armando explained that to me very carefully...

Japanese: すべて、カクゴの上で 協力させていただきました。
English: I knew the risk and I willingly cooperated anyway.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: 判決の前に‥‥‥何か 言っておくことはありますか？
English: Before I hand down my verdict, is there anything you'd like to say?

Japanese: それでは‥‥ひとつだけ。 成歩堂さんに。
English: Well, there is one thing. I'd like to say something to Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたに‥‥おわびしなければ ならないことがあります。
English: I want to... I want to apologize to you.

Japanese: お‥‥おわび‥‥ ぼくに、ですか？
English: Apologize? To me? For what?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥ ６年前の、あの事件です。
English: For the case 6 years ago, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥思い出しました。
English: I just remembered...

Japanese: たしか‥‥交際していた恋人に、 あなたが毒殺されたのでしたな？
English: Weren't you poisoned by your own lover?

Japanese: ‥‥セイカクではありませんが、 おおむね、そんな感じです。
English: ...Not exactly, but yeah, something like that.

Japanese: じつは‥‥６年たった今でも、 まだ信じられないんです。
English: Even now... 6 years later, I can hardly believe it.

Japanese: 彼女が‥‥ぼくに、 あんなことをした、なんて‥‥
English: She was going to do it... She was planning to kill me...

Japanese: ムリもありませんね。
English: It's not all that surprising.

Japanese: おふたりは‥‥お互いのことを よく知らなかったのですから。
English: The two of you... You hardly knew each other.

Japanese: ど‥‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Huh...? What do you mean?

Japanese: あなたと、お姉さまは‥‥
English: You and my sister...

Japanese: たった２回しか 会ったことがないのです。
English: You only met twice.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh.

Japanese: に‥‥“２回しか” 会ったことがない‥‥？
English: W-We only met t-twice?

Japanese: お姉さまとあなたが初めて 会ったのは、あの運命の日‥‥
English: The first time you met was on that fateful day...

Japanese: 裁判所で、神乃木弁護士さまに 毒を飲ませた、あのときでした。
English: The day she poisoned Mr. Armando in the cafeteria of this very courthouse.

Japanese: そして、次におふたりが会ったのは ‥‥それから、半年後のこと。
English: The next time you met her was... six months later.

Japanese: お姉さまが、あなたを殺害する ために、カゼ薬を盗みだして‥‥
English: You met her again on the day that she stole your cold medicine...

Japanese: 呑田　菊三さんが亡くなった、 あの日だったのです。
English: ...and Doug Swallow was killed.

Japanese: い、いやいや！ そんな‥‥そんなハズはない！
English: N-N-No way! It just... It can't be true!

Japanese: ‥‥だって、その半年のあいだ、 ぼくたちは、ずっと‥‥
English: I mean, during our whole relationship, we were...

Japanese: その、半年のあいだ‥‥
English: For those 6 months...

Japanese: あなたが《美柳　ちなみ》だと 思っていたのは‥‥
English: ...the woman that you thought was Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: お姉さまではなかったのです。
English: ...wasn't actually my sister.

Japanese: （み、《美柳　ちなみ》が‥‥ 彼女じゃなかった‥‥？）
English: (Huh? It wasn't Dahlia...?)

Japanese: 申しわけありませんでした。 リュウ‥‥ちゃん。
English: I hope one day you can forgive me... Feenie.

Japanese: も‥‥‥もしかして‥‥‥？
English: You... Y-Y-You mean...?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり、です。
English: That's right...

Japanese: 半年間‥‥あなたを あざむいておりました。
English: I lied to you... for 6 months.

Japanese: い‥‥‥いったい、なぜ！ そんな、入れ替わりを！
English: B-But why...!? Why would you do such a thing?

Japanese: あの日、あの小ビンが 成歩堂さんの手にわたってから‥‥
English: Ever since she gave you the bottle that day...

Japanese: お姉さまは、一刻も早く それを取り戻したかったのです。
English: ...my sister was trying to get it back as soon as she possibly could.

Japanese: でも‥‥警察の取り調べや 監視などもあったようで‥‥
English: Because of the police investigation and their surveillance...

Japanese: お姉さまは、しばらく 自由に動けませんでした。
English: ...she couldn't move about freely.

Japanese: だから‥‥あなたが？
English: So that's why you...?

Japanese: お姉さまは‥‥最初から、 サイアクの場合を考えていました。
English: My sister... From the beginning, she was prepared for the worst.

Japanese: さ、“サイアクの場合”‥‥？
English: P-Prepared for the worst?

Japanese: どんなキッカケで、あなたが 真相を知ってしまうかもしれない。
English: She thought that you might somehow discover the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、危険な存在である あなたを、すぐにでも‥‥
English: That's why she was always ready to deal with you at a moment's notice...

Japanese: （殺そうと考えた、ワケか‥‥）
English: (You mean she was ready to kill me, don't you...?)

Japanese: 私‥‥これ以上、お姉さまに 罪を重ねてほしくなかった。
English: She already had so much to answer for, I didn't want any more sins on her soul.

Japanese: 必死におねがいして、 待っていただきました。
English: I begged her not to do it, and she agreed to give me a chance.

Japanese: それで‥‥あなたが ぼくの前にあらわれた。
English: And that's why... you came to me?

Japanese: 彼女のかわりに、あの ペンダントを取り戻すために‥‥
English: You came to get the bottle pendant back from me in her place?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、やっぱり‥‥。 私は、ダメだったんです。
English: But I couldn't get you to give it back...

Japanese: 《ペンダントを取り戻す》‥‥ たったそれだけのことだったのに。
English: I failed at something even as simple as that.

Japanese: どうしても‥‥それができないまま 半年がすぎてしまいました。
English: Six months passed and I still couldn't get it back from you.

Japanese: そして、お姉さまは‥‥ ついに、待てなくなったのですね。
English: Finally, my sister couldn't wait any longer.

Japanese: あの日のことは‥‥何も私に 相談してくださいませんでした。
English: She didn't consult me about her plans for you that day.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことは、 初めてだったのですが。
English: ...It was the first time that had ever happened.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ それは、ちょっと妙ですね。
English: That was a bit strange, wasn't it?

Japanese: それまでの犯行計画は、すべて あなたも聞かされていたのに‥‥
English: Up until that day, you two were partners in crime, and she would confer with you...

Japanese: おそらく‥‥お姉さまは、 気づいていたのでしょう。
English: I think... she must have noticed.

Japanese: ‥‥？ あの。“何に”‥‥ですか？
English: Noticed what?

Japanese: 私の気持ちに、です。
English: My feelings for you.

Japanese: もし‥‥あなたの殺害計画を 知ってしまったら‥‥私。
English: If I had found out she was planning to kill you...

Japanese: 何があっても、それだけは 止めようとしたでしょうから。
English: ...I would have done whatever was necessary to stop her.

Japanese: たとえ‥‥そのせいで、私や お姉さまが、死ぬことになっても。
English: Even if it meant her life... or mine.

Japanese: あ、あやめさん‥‥
English: I-Iris...!

Japanese: ‥‥半年のあいだ、あなたと いっしょにいて‥‥
English: After spending half a year by your side...

Japanese: 私の気持ちは‥‥ 変わっていったのです。
English: ...my feelings towards you... They changed.

Japanese: １つだけ、言わせてください。
English: I have something to say to you, too.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: あなたは、やっぱり‥‥ ぼくの思ったとおりのひとでした。
English: You really are the person I always thought you were.

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》が 有罪判決を受けてからも‥‥
English: Even after Dahlia Hawthorne was found guilty...

Japanese: それだけは、信じていました。
English: I still believed in you.

Japanese: ありがとう‥‥
English: Thank you.

Japanese: 今まで、いったい何杯の《闇》を あおったか、おぼえていねえ‥‥
English: How many cups of darkness have I drank over the years? Even I don't know...

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: I'll tell you though...

Japanese: 今日、この１杯こそは‥‥ 何よりも、すばらしい。
English: Right now, this one here is the greatest cup I think I've ever had.

Japanese: アンタもそう思うだろう？ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Don't you think so... Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ええ。 ‥‥そうかもしれません。
English: Yeah... I think you're right.

Japanese: この法廷で裁かれていたのは、 あくまで被害者の《殺害》です。
English: The purpose of this trial was to rule on the murder of the victim, Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: これから先、被告人は再び、 他の罪状で審理を受けるでしょう。
English: At some point, I expect you will be tried for your role as an accomplice in this case.

Japanese: ‥‥わかっております。
English: ...I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: それでは！　罪状を《殺人》に かぎって、今。判決を下します！
English: Very well! On the charge of murder, I hereby find the defendant...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Court is now adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　４時５１分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: February 10, 4:51 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: （‥‥これで、 すべて終わったわけか‥‥）
English: (So I guess it's all over...)

Japanese: （本当に‥‥本当に、 これでよかったのか‥‥？）
English: (The way everything ended... Was justice really served...?)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのために命を かけてくれた人を、ぼくは‥‥）
English: (The man who risked his life to save Maya is being sent to prison by my own hand...)

Japanese: もちろん‥‥ これでよかったのよ。
English: Of course justice was served.

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥さすが、なるほどくんね。
English: ...I'm proud of you, Phoenix.

Japanese: 今日の弁護‥‥ まさに、最高だったわ。
English: Your defense was... truly brilliant.

Japanese: で、でも！　‥‥ぼくは 神乃木さんを救えなかった！
English: B-But I couldn't save Mr. Armando!

Japanese: 千尋さんのことを、あんなに 想ってくれていた人なのに‥‥
English: The man who cared so deeply for you...

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。それはちがうわ、 なるほどくん。
English: You're wrong, Phoenix.

Japanese: あなたは、神乃木さんを 救ってくれたの。
English: You did save Diego.

Japanese: ‥‥彼を救うことのできる、 ただ１つの方法で。
English: You saved him in the only way possible.

Japanese: それが‥‥ あの《判決》だったんですか？
English: You mean... with that verdict...?

Japanese: あなたにも‥‥ いつか、わかる日が来るわ。
English: I think one day, you'll understand, too.

Japanese: ただ、これだけは忘れないで。
English: Phoenix, I want you to remember one thing...

Japanese: あなたは今日、弁護士として 最高のシゴトをしたのよ。
English: You were as good out there today as any defense lawyer could ever hope to be.

Japanese: もう‥‥私から言うことは、 何もないわ。
English: There's nothing more you can learn from me.

Japanese: ち、千尋さん‥‥！
English: M-Mia...!

Japanese: あなたは‥‥私にできなかった ことを、やりとげてくれたの。
English: You've accomplished something I wasn't able to.

Japanese: お礼を言うわ。 ‥‥本当に、ありがとう。
English: I owe you a great deal. ...Thank you.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: また‥‥いつか、会いましょう。 なるほどくん。
English: I'm sure we'll meet again... someday... Phoenix.

Japanese: （‥‥いろんな事件があった‥‥）
English: (I've handled lots of cases and seen a lot of things...)

Japanese: （そのたびに‥‥ぼくは、 自分自身に問いかけてきた）
English: (And along this journey, I've found myself asking just one question...)

Japanese: （本当の《弁護》とは いったい、なんなのか‥‥？）
English: (What does it really mean to defend someone?)

Japanese: （‥‥今日の判決が、ひとつの 《答え》だったのだろうか‥‥）
English: (...I suppose today's case produced one possible answer.)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Nick...

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: M-Maya...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥お姉ちゃんの 言ったとおりだったよ。
English: I guess it's just like my sis said...

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥？
English: Your sister...? Mia...?

Japanese: ‥‥あの夜、修験堂で お姉ちゃんに相談したとき‥‥
English: That night, when I channeled Mia to get her advice on what to do...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、メモに こう書いてくれたの。
English: This is what she wrote back in my notebook.

Japanese: なるほどくんなら、きっと みんなを助けてくれる‥‥って！
English: "Don't worry. Phoenix will save everyone in the end."

Japanese: で、でも‥‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: もう！　なんで暗いカオ、 してるかなあ。
English: Come on! Cut it out with that gloomy face!

Japanese: あたしも、お姉ちゃんも‥‥ きっと、あやめさんも。
English: Can't you see? Me, sis, and I'm sure Iris, too...

Japanese: こーんなに、なるほどくんに カンシャしてるんだよ！
English: We owe you for everything you have done for us, Nick!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: （どうして‥‥きみは、 そんなに明るいんだろう‥‥？）
English: (How? How can you be so bright and chipper after all that's happened...?)

Japanese: （ヒドい目にあって‥‥ おかあさんまで、亡くしたのに）
English: (You were brutally attacked... You even saw your mother murdered...)

Japanese: いてててェ！
English: Yeeeowch!

Japanese: か‥‥‥狩魔　冥ッ！
English: F-Franziska!

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだアマいわね、 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Still a softy as always, Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: よくやったな‥‥成歩堂。
English: Excellent work, Wright.

Japanese: あれ！　みつるぎ検事！ いつ、帰ってきたんですか？
English: Huh!? Mr. Edgeworth!? When did you get back?

Japanese: （そうか‥‥真宵ちゃんは 知らないんだっけ）
English: (Oh, that's right... I guess no one filled her in on that.)

Japanese: ‥‥御剣と狩魔検事はね。 ぼくを助けてくれたんだ。
English: Edgeworth and Franziska have actually been helping me.

Japanese: なるほどくんを‥‥？
English: Helping you...!?

Japanese: １日目の、審理‥‥ このふたりがいなければ‥‥
English: If these two hadn't been here on the first day of the trial...

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、 どうにもならなかったよ。
English: ...the defense wouldn't have gotten anywhere.

Japanese: ‥‥ふーん。 で。そのとき、なるほどくんは？
English: Wow... But where were you, Nick?

Japanese: 川に落っこちておぼれた上、 カゼで寝こんだと聞いているわ。
English: I heard he fell into a river and caught a nasty cold which forced him to sleep all day.

Japanese: 熱にうなされて、あやめさんの ずきんをかぶってフルえていたな。
English: Yes, he laid in bed shivering from his fever with Iris's hood pulled over his head.

Japanese: かー。情けないね、なるほどくん。 まだまだ、修行が足りないよ！
English: Oh, ouch! Talk about embarrassing, Nick. You definitely need more training!

Japanese: 本当に、ありがとう！ みつるぎ検事に‥‥冥さん！
English: Anyway, thank you, Mr. Edgeworth! And you too... um... Franziska!
I don't suppose...

Japanese: オレの居場所なんて、 どこにもないんだ‥‥
English: ...there's room for me in this group hug, is there?

Japanese: あ！　ヤッパリさん！
English: Oh! Larry!

Japanese: なんでおまえが そんなカオしてるんだよ！
English: What's with the, uh... longer than usual face?

Japanese: オレ‥‥生まれたときに もう、気づいてたんだよなァ。
English: I realized something when I was reborn...

Japanese: ‥‥ヤハリさんちのマサシくんは、 ココロの底から役立たずだ、って。
English: ...I realized that Larry was never of any use to anyone. Not even once.

Japanese: そ、そんなコトないですよ！ ね。なるほどくん！
English: Th-That's not true! Right, Nick!?

Japanese: え！　‥‥ぼ、ぼくかよ！
English: What? You're asking me!?

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントか！　成歩堂ォォ！ オレ‥‥役に立ったのかな！
English: Well, Nick!? Is it true!? I've got a place in this world, right!?

Japanese: え？　ええと‥‥そうだな。
English: Huh? Oh, um... Y-Yeah, of course.

Japanese: やっぱり、オレ‥‥死ぬわ。
English: I knew it. Everyone would be better off if I were gone for good.

Japanese: いやいやいや！ えーと、その‥‥アレだよ！
English: No, no, no! Umm, I... Oh yeah!

Japanese: おまえが描いてくれた、似顔絵！ ‥‥あれは、なかなか似てたな。
English: Those portraits you painted! They were... really good.

Japanese: ‥‥な、御剣！
English: Isn't that right, Edgeworth!?

Japanese: え！　‥‥わ、私かッ！
English: What!? M-Me?

Japanese: そ、そうなのかオイ！ ‥‥御剣よォォ！
English: Why are you making that face!? Huh, Edgey!?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥あれは、たしかに‥‥ ソックリと言えなくもない。
English: Oh, well, um, yes, indeed... I certainly can't say that it's lacking resemblance.

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントか！ ホントかい、メイちゃん！
English: Do you really mean it!? What about you, Franzy? Did I draw you well, too!?

Japanese: ‥‥ぎゃはアッ！
English: Waaah!

Japanese: 私の美しさを、クレヨンごときで 表現できるわけが‥‥
English: My beauty can't possibly be captured by a mere crayon!

Japanese: ‥‥じろ。
English: ...*glare*

Japanese: まあ、そのドリョクは 認めてやらないでもないわ。
English: Nevertheless, I recognize the effort you put into it and that's worth something.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥メイちゃん！ あのヤクソク、ホンキにするぜ！
English: So then you'll do it!? Like you promised!?

Japanese: 《メイちゃんのムチムチ大冒険》の モデルになってくれるって‥‥
English: You're going to model for my next picture book, "Franzy's Whip Lash Splash"?

Japanese: ぎゃははアッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥調子に乗るなッ！
English: Don't get carried away!

Japanese: いやー、オレ。似顔絵だけは 自信あるんだよなァ。
English: Well, how about that! I guess painting portraits is the only thing I'm good at.

Japanese: ハルミちゃんの絵も、 なかなかのデキだったんだぜ！
English: The painting of Pearl was pretty darn good too, if you ask me!

Japanese: あれ‥‥？
English: Huh...?

Japanese: そういえば‥‥あの少女の 姿が見あたらないな。
English: Now that you mention it... I haven't seen her around.

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥ （どこへ行ったんだろう‥‥）
English: Pearls... (Where could she have gone?)

Japanese: （いつもなら、まっ先に真宵ちゃん のところへ飛んでくるのに‥‥）
English: (Normally she would have made a beeline for Maya...)

Japanese: あ‥‥。あたし、 ちょっと探してくるね！
English: Oh... I'll go look for her! Be right back!

Japanese: おーい！　はみちゃあん‥‥
English: Hey Pearly...!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: ...Wright.

Japanese: キミは‥‥フシギに 思っているようだな。
English: You seem to be uncharacteristically puzzled.

Japanese: 真宵くんが‥‥なぜ、 あんなに元気なのか‥‥？
English: I suspect you are wondering how Maya can be so cheerful despite all that has happened?

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ああ。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥ 私にも、リカイできないわ。
English: To be honest... I can't understand it either.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Franziska...)

Japanese: （狩魔　冥も‥‥お父さんを 亡くしているんだよな‥‥）
English: (That's right... She lost her father fairly recently as well...)

Japanese: 私には‥‥‥ わかるような気がする。
English: I think I understand how she feels.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、真宵くんは 知っているのだと思う。
English: Maya is a much wiser person than she appears, and I think she realizes something...

Japanese: 今こそ‥‥彼女は、強くなければ ならないのだ、と。
English: Now is exactly the time when she needs to be as strong as she can.

Japanese: ど、どういうこと？ “今こそ”‥‥
English: Wh-What do you mean by, "Now is exactly the time"...?

Japanese: この事件で傷ついたのは、 真宵くんだけではない。
English: Maya wasn't the only one that was badly wounded by this incident.

Japanese: むしろ‥‥もっと、ひどく ココロを痛めた人物がいる。
English: In fact, there was someone that was hurt far more deeply than she.

Japanese: その《人物》のために‥‥ 彼女は、決して泣かないのだろう。
English: I believe it's for that person that Maya is trying her best not to cry.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんよりも 深く傷ついた《人物》‥‥）
English: (Someone who was hurt more deeply than Maya...)

Japanese: ‥‥御剣‥‥。ぼくにも、 わかるような気がするよ。
English: Edgeworth... I think I'm starting to understand, too.

Japanese: いててててェ！
English: Owwwww!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！　教えなさいッ！
English: Then tell me, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は‥‥だれのために 強くなれるのか‥‥？
English: Who is Maya Fey being strong for...?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことかしら？ 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: ...Why did you just show me that, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 今の証拠‥‥ イミがわからないわ！
English: I have no idea what purpose this is supposed to serve!

Japanese: あ、あれ‥‥？
English: H-Huh?

Japanese: フッ‥‥。 まあ、いいだろう。成歩堂‥‥
English: Heh. Well, don't worry, Wright...

Japanese: 私の考えも、当たっているとは かぎらない。
English: I'm not certain that my guess is entirely correct either.

Japanese: 《真実》は‥‥真宵くんの ココロの中にしか、存在しない。
English: Only Maya can really say what the truth is inside her heart.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (It looks like...)

Japanese: （ぼくは、真宵ちゃんを リカイできなかったみたいだな）
English: (I still have a lot to learn about Maya.)

Japanese: いてててててェ！
English: Aaaaaargh!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥
English: Just as I thought...

Japanese: まだまだね、成歩堂　龍一！
English: You have a lot to learn about women, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　春美‥‥
English: Pearl Fey...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、一生懸命 指示書にしたがおうとしたのは‥‥
English: The poor kid. After all, the reason that she worked so hard to follow the instructions...

Japanese: おかあさん‥‥綾里　キミ子を 信じていたからだ。
English: ...was because she loved and believed in her mother, Morgan.

Japanese: 《綾里家のため》‥‥ そのコトバを、あの子は信じた。
English: "It's for the good of the Fey clan"... I'm sure she believed in every last word.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのためになる‥‥って、 あんなにうれしそうだった。
English: She thought she was doing it for Maya... That's why she was so happy.

Japanese: ‥‥それほど、真宵ちゃんが 大好きだったんだ‥‥。
English: It shows how truly devoted she is to Maya.

Japanese: しかし‥‥あの子の行動が 生んだのは、悲劇だった。
English: But it's a cruel irony that it was her exuberance that led to this tragedy.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、母親を失い‥‥ みずからも、危機におちいった。
English: Maya Fey's mother was killed and Maya herself was put into the deepest peril imaginable.

Japanese: （だからこそ‥‥、真宵ちゃんは 泣かないのだろう）
English: (And that's exactly why Maya is putting on a brave face...)

Japanese: （あの子が‥‥エガオを 取り戻す、その日まで‥‥）
English: (She's doing it for Pearls's sake... Until she can see her smile again...)

Japanese: ‥‥おッ、いたいた！ みんな、おそろいッスね！
English: Oh, hey! So this is where you all were!

Japanese: いやー、今日の裁判は、 いつにも増して
English: Wow, looks like we've got quite the bunch here toda--

Japanese: ぎゃあああああッス！
English: YEOWCH!

Japanese: ‥‥なな、ナニするッスか いきなり！
English: Wh-What was that for, sir!?

Japanese: 悪いわね、ヒゲ。
English: Sorry about that, Scruffy.

Japanese: ‥‥考えるより先に、 このムチが走っていたわ。
English: ...My whip just seems to have a mind of its own.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたんですか？ イトノコ刑事。
English: What's up, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: どうしたも、こうしたも‥‥ 勝訴ッス！　お祝いッス！
English: Oh, you know. This and that. Anyway, congrats on your win!

Japanese: 今夜は、パーッとやるッス！ 自分がゴチソウするッス！
English: Let's go out tonight, pal! Dinner's on me!

Japanese: 今月も、給料がちょっぴり 下がったッスけど、気にしねッス！
English: My salary's just sorta kinda gone down by a teeny-weeny bit, but it's alright!

Japanese: 一流フランス料理のレストランに、 キッチリ予約を入れといたッス！
English: I made reservations at a first class French restaurant tonight!

Japanese: ぎゃああああああッス！
English: AAAAAAH!

Japanese: ヒゲにしては、よくやったわ。 ‥‥ねぎらいのムチよ。
English: Pretty good work, Scruffy. That whip was your reward.

Japanese: あの‥‥イトノコ刑事。
English: Umm... Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: “一流フランス料理のレストラン” って、もしかして‥‥？
English: You said a first class French restaurant... You don't mean...?

Japanese: モチロン‥‥《吐麗美庵》ッス！
English: Trés Bien, where else!?

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew it... We're doomed.)

Japanese: さ。さ。みんなで行くッス！ マコクンも待ってるッス！
English: Come on! Let's go, everyone! Can't keep Maggey waiting, pal!

Japanese: そこのショボくれてる アンタも行くッス！
English: Hey! You! Crybaby! You're invited, too!

Japanese: ああ‥‥オレはいいや。
English: Oh... Forget about me.

Japanese: 負け犬たちの山小屋で、 ハルミちゃんとイモ食うから。
English: Pearl and I will be at the losers' shack eating potatoes.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥ おそいな、真宵ちゃん）
English: (You know... Maya is taking an awfully long time to get back...)

Japanese: （‥‥春美ちゃんを 探しにいったままだぞ‥‥）
English: (She's still out looking for Pearls...)

Japanese: あ‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: Oh... Maya... What's wrong?

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥ どうしよう‥‥
English: Nick... What do I do...?

Japanese: はみちゃん‥‥ どこにもいないの！
English: Pearly... I can't find her anywhere!
Huh...?

Japanese: き、きっと‥‥ おウチに帰ったッスよ！
English: I'll bet she just went back home. Th-That's all!

Japanese: ‥‥倉院の里に 電話してみたんだけど‥‥
English: I thought so too, so I called the village...

Japanese: なんの連絡もない、って。 そんなこと、今までなかったのに！
English: But no one has heard from her! This has never happened before!

Japanese: やはり‥‥ 深く傷ついているのだろう。
English: As I figured... She has been badly hurt by this incident.

Japanese: キミに対して‥‥セキニンを 感じているのかもしれない。
English: She feels responsible for the tragedy that has befallen you, Maya.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ はみちゃんのせいじゃないのに！
English: B-But... None of this was her fault!

Japanese: ‥‥あたし‥‥ どうすればいいの‥‥？
English: What... What should I do...?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: え！　‥‥な、なんスか！
English: Yeah, what is it, pal?

Japanese: みんなを連れて、先に 行っててもらえますか。
English: Could you guys go on ahead?

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、アンタは！
English: But... But what about you!?

Japanese: ぼくと、真宵ちゃんは‥‥
English: Maya and I will...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんをむかえに 行ってきます。
English: We'll join you guys once we find Pearls.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥！
English: Nick...!

Japanese: 大丈夫ですよ、イトノコ刑事。
English: Don't worry about us, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 少し遅れるけど‥‥ かならず、行きますから。
English: We may be a little late... But we'll definitely be there.

Japanese: ‥‥なんと言っても 今夜は、お祝いですからね。
English: We have a lot of celebrating to do tonight, and I wouldn't miss it for the world.

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥その。アンタは‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, but you're the...

Japanese: ぎゃあああああああッス！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: いいわ、成歩堂　龍一。 ‥‥先に行ってる。
English: Very well, Phoenix Wright. We'll go on ahead.

Japanese: 待っているぞ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Don't keep us waiting, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。
English: We won't...

Japanese: で‥‥でも！　はみちゃん、 行くところなんて‥‥
English: B-But where should we look!? Where could Pearly have gone...?

Japanese: 行こう、真宵ちゃん。
English: Let's go, Maya.

Japanese: ココロ当たりがあるんだ。 ‥‥１カ所だけ、ね。
English: There's only one place I can think of that Pearls might have gone to...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 10 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: 葉桜院‥‥
English: Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: たぶん‥‥春美ちゃんにとって、 忘れられない場所だと思うんだ。
English: For Pearls, I bet this is a very important place.

Japanese: すべてが始まった場所、だから。
English: After all, it's where this whole incident started.

Japanese: なーんだ。もう来ちゃったの、 アンタたち。
English: What's this? You're all back again so soon?

Japanese: び‥‥ビキニさん‥‥
English: Sister Bikini?

Japanese: 今夜は、あの子とふたりで カレーでも食べようと思ったのに。
English: I thought we'd be eating mashed potatoes alone tonight.

Japanese: は、はみちゃん！ やっぱり、ここに‥‥？
English: S-So she's here? Pearly is here...?

Japanese: ‥‥修験堂にいるよ。 早く、行っておあげなさい。
English: She's in the Training Hall. Why don't you hurry along and go see her?

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: O-OK!

Japanese: ‥‥いないね、はみちゃん‥‥
English: Pearly's not here...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！ ‥‥あの、掛け軸‥‥
English: Maya! The hanging scroll!

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: キレイになってるよ‥‥
English: Someone cleaned it off...

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、だな‥‥）
English: (It's got to be Pearls...)

Japanese: あああああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 真宵さまっ！
English: Mystic Maya!

Japanese: は‥‥はみちゃん‥‥！
English: P-Pearly...!

Japanese: もう‥‥
English: Why...

Japanese: あたしをおいて行っちゃ、やだよ。
English: Why did you just leave like that?

Japanese: 真宵さま‥‥ まよいさまあああああっ！
English: Mystic Maya... Mystic Mayaaaaaaaaaaaaa!

Japanese: わたくし‥‥もう一生、おふたりに お会いしないと決めていたのです。
English: I swore... I swore that I would never trouble the two of you ever again.

Japanese: だって‥‥わたくしのせいで、 真宵さまは‥‥おかあさまを！
English: Because... It's all my fault that... Mystic Maya's mother...!

Japanese: だから、ここへ‥‥？
English: That's why you came here?

Japanese: せめて‥‥真宵さまのために、 お祈りしようと思ったのです。
English: It's the least I could do... To pray for your happiness!

Japanese: いいんだよ、はみちゃん。
English: You don't have to do that, Pearly.

Japanese: だって‥‥はみちゃんは ちっとも、悪くないから。
English: It wasn't your fault. You didn't do anything wrong.

Japanese: おかあさんがいなくなったのは、 たしかに悲しいけど‥‥
English: Of course I'm sad that my mother is gone...

Japanese: でも、なんでだろうね。 あたし‥‥しあわせだよ。
English: But... how do I say this? I... I'm still happy.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ ウソをつかないでください！
English: Y-You don't have to lie just to make me feel better!

Japanese: ううん‥‥ ホントだよ。だって‥‥
English: No, really... It's true...

Japanese: あたしが今、ここにいるのは、 みんなのおかげなんだから。
English: The only reason I'm still here at all is thanks to everyone who was there for me.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、おかあさん、 神乃木さん、なるほどくん‥‥
English: My sis, my mother, Mr. Armando, Nick...

Japanese: そして‥‥、はみちゃん。
English: And... you.

Japanese: だれか１人でもいなかったら‥‥ あたしはきっと、死んでいた。
English: If even one of you weren't there, I'm sure I wouldn't still be alive right now.

Japanese: だから‥‥あたし。 強くならなきゃいけないんだよね。
English: That's why... I have to be strong.

Japanese: みんなが助けてくれた命だから。
English: For all the people that were there for me when I needed them.

Japanese: あたしには‥‥ それぐらいしかできないから。
English: That's all I can really do.

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵さま‥‥
English: M-Mystic Maya...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥舞子さまの 娘さんだね‥‥
English: I'm impressed... You truly are the daughter of Mystic Misty.

Japanese: ‥‥毘忌尼さん‥‥！
English: Sister Bikini...!

Japanese: アンタのおかあさま‥‥ 舞子さまは、強かった。
English: Your mother, Mystic Misty, was a strong woman indeed.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥オバサンに、 こんなことを言ってたよ。
English: I want to tell you what she said to me that night.

Japanese: 倉院の名を汚したわたくしに、娘に カオを会わせる資格はありません。
English: After dishonoring the good name of Kurain, I don't have the right to face my daughter.

Japanese: でも‥‥わたくしはいつでも、 あの子を想いつづけています。
English: But still... Maya is always in my thoughts.

Japanese: そう‥‥この身に離さず、 きっと、死ぬまで‥‥
English: It's true... She'll always be with me, until the day I die...

Japanese: 舞子さまが強かったのは‥‥ アンタがいたから、なんだね。
English: Your spirit was with her... That's why your mother was so strong.

Japanese: きっと‥‥最後の瞬間まで、 後悔はなかったはずだよ。
English: Even at the end... I'm sure she had no regrets.

Japanese: “この身に離さず、死ぬまで” ‥‥か。
English: "She'll always be with me, until the day I die", huh...

Japanese: たしか‥‥家元には、 こんなキマリがあったね。
English: There's a rule or something all Masters are to follow, isn't there?

Japanese: 死ぬまで、肌身はなさず‥‥ 守り抜かなければならない。
English: To never take the charm off until the day you die...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 家元さまの護符‥‥
English: Th-That's the Master's Talisman...

Japanese: 舞子さんにとって、守り抜かな ければならなかったもの‥‥
English: The thing that Misty kept by her heart and would never take off...

Japanese: それは、こんな入れ物じゃない。 その、中身なんじゃないかな。
English: It wasn't the container that was important. Rather, it was the contents.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥！
English: Th-That's...!

Japanese: 写真‥‥ですか‥‥？
English: A photo?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
‥‥おかあさん‥‥
English: ...Mother...

Japanese: （命あるものが、自分を守るため 戦うのは、当然のことだ）
English: (It's only natural for living creatures to fight to protect their own lives.)

Japanese: （“人間”だからこそ‥‥ だれかのために、戦える）
English: (But what makes us human is that we fight for others.)

Japanese: （‥‥だれかのために、 どれだけ強くなれるか‥‥？）
English: (But who do you fight for? How hard must you fight...?)

Japanese: （それこそが人間の価値）
English: (That's the true measure of what human life is worth.)

Japanese: （弁護士は、つねにその価値を 試される、戦士なんだ‥‥）
English: (We defense attorneys are warriors who are constantly challenged by that question.)

Japanese: （戦いの末に結ばれた《絆》は、 たとえ、断ち切られても‥‥）
English: (Even when the battle is over, and the bonds that connect us are severed...)

Japanese: （かならず、よみがえる。 ‥‥何度でも）
English: (We always return... Time and time again.)

Japanese: （千尋さん、真宵ちゃん、 春美ちゃん、神乃木さん‥‥）
English: (Mia, Maya, Pearls, Mr. Armando...)

Japanese: （‥‥そして、真宵ちゃんの おかあさんが‥‥）
English: (...and Maya's mother, too...)

Japanese: （それを教えてくれた）
English: (I learned that... from all of them.)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥そろそろ行こうか。 みんなが待ってる。
English: Well... Shall we get going? Everyone is waiting.

Japanese: 今日は、すべてに決着がついた 記念日だよ。
English: This is a day to remember. A day when a lot of things were finally put to rest.

Japanese: 思いっきり、お祝いしないとね。
English: I think we should celebrate what we've overcome today.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥ わたくしは、もう‥‥
English: B-But... I-I still can't...

Japanese: ‥‥行っといで。
English: ...Oh, go on, sweetie.

Japanese: 修行なんか、 いつだってできるからね。
English: You can come back for training anytime.

Japanese: は‥‥‥はいっ！
English: Um... OK!

Japanese: よおし！　あたしも、 もう一度、出なおしだね！
English: Alright! I'm going to make a brand new start, too!

Japanese: 毘忌尼さん！　あたし、 かならず、帰ってくるから。
English: Sister Bikini! I'll be back for more training! I promise!

Japanese: わかってるよ。未来の家元さま だからって、ヨウシャしないよ。
English: I know, and I won't go easy on you just because you're the future Master.

Japanese: ‥‥予約３人ぶん用意して、 待ってるからね。
English: I'll make sure to prepare reservations for three for when you come back!

Japanese: わはは。わははは。わはわははは
English: Wa ha ha ho ho ho hee hee hee!

Japanese: よおおし。 今日は食べるよ、はみちゃん。
English: Alright! We're going to have a great feast today, Pearly.

Japanese: 修行はね。体力勝負だから。
English: You know why? Because training is a battle of endurance!

Japanese: はいっ！　真宵さま！ わたくし‥‥食べますともっ！
English: OK, Mystic Maya! I... I'll eat lots and lots of food tonight!

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。ちょっと 聞きたいことがあるんだけど。
English: ...Um, you know. There's one thing I don't get... and probably don't want to, but...

Japanese: なに？　なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: 修行の予約はいいけど‥‥
English: Reservations for training is fine and all...

Japanese: なんで“３人ぶん”なんだ？
English: But why for three?

Japanese: そんなの、決まってるでしょ！
English: Come on! What do you think?

Japanese: モチロン‥‥なるほどくんも いっしょだよ！
English: You're one of us, Nick! Next time you can train right alongside us!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 待ってるわよ、アンタ。
English: I'll be waiting for you.

Japanese: オバサン、たっぷり かわいがってあげるからねえ‥‥！
English: Sister Bikini will take special care of you!

Japanese: え。え。
English: Huh? Huh?

Japanese: いっちょ、やるよ！ なるほどくん。
English: It'll be great, Nick!

Japanese: モチロン、 《スペシャル・コース》でね！
English: We're going to do the Special Course, naturally!

Japanese: え。え。え。
English: Huh? Huh? Huh?

Japanese: そうですとも！ だって、なるほどくんは‥‥
English: That's a great idea! After all, Mr. Nick...

Japanese: 真宵さまのためならば、 いつも火の中、水の中ですから！
English: ...you'd do anything for Mystic Maya, wouldn't you? Even walk on hot coals, right?

Japanese: しっかり、食べておこうね！ なるほどくん！
English: We'll have a nice, big meal before we come next time! Right, Nick!?

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。
English: ...You know...

Japanese: ヒトコトだけ‥‥ 言わせてもらってもいいかな。
English: I was wondering if I can say just one little thing...

Japanese: うん。モチロン、いいよ！
English: Sure! Of course you can!

Japanese: わたくし、大好きです！ あのコトバ‥‥
English: Oh, I love this part! I can't wait to hear it...!

Japanese: オバサンも、ゾクゾクしちゃうワ。
English: I'm getting goosebumps, too!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥‥ おコトバにあまえて。
English: Well then... Here goes nothing...

Japanese: ■企画・脚本・監督 巧　　舟
English: ■Planning/Script/Director Shu Takumi

Japanese: さあ、これからは あたしがシッカリしないとね！
English: I'll really have to work extra hard now!

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の家元に、 成歩堂法律事務所のカゲの所長。
English: Master of Kurain and the office manager of Wright & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: はみちゃんとなるほどくんの お姉さんとして、がんばらないと！
English: And I have to be a good big sister to Pearly and Nick, too!

Japanese: さすがにもう、閉じこめられたり さらわれるのは、カンベンだなあ。
English: Well, as long as I'm not locked up or captured or something like that.

Japanese: ■デザイン・原画 岩元　辰郎
English: ■Design/Art Tatsurou Iwamoto

Japanese: やっぱり、なるほどくんは 真宵さまの王子さまなのですね！
English: So it's true! Mr. Nick really is Mystic Maya's knight in shining armor!

Japanese: あの《スペシャル・コース》を 最後まで添いとげたのですから！
English: He went through with the Special Course, all the way to the end!

Japanese: そういえば、ここにはマボロシの 《ウルトラ・コース》があるとか。
English: Actually, I heard there's a legendary, extra-special Ultra Course here, too.

Japanese: おふたりのために、ちょっと わたくしが、ご予約を‥‥。
English: I think I'll surprise the two of them by making them a secret reservation...

Japanese: ■グラフィック・チーフ 村本　シュウイチ
English: ■Graphics Lead Shuichi Muramoto

Japanese: マコクンから、コートを プレゼントしてもらったッス！
English: Maggey bought me a brand new coat as a present, pal!

Japanese: 若がえった気分ッス！ もう一生、脱がないッス！
English: I feel 10 years younger! I'll never take it off!

Japanese: うーん。でも、なんかイマイチ、 センパイっぽくないッス！
English: Yes, but somehow you just don't seem the same!

Japanese: やっぱり、うすよごれていたほうが 《刑事（デカ）》っぽいのかなあ。
English: I guess a dirty, shabby old overcoat is just more detective-like, sir.

Japanese: 大丈夫！　アイのために、男は 何度でも、うすよごれるッス！
English: Don't worry about it! In the name of love, a man will soil himself silly!

Japanese: ■グラフィック 武田　清子 寺本　千秋
English: ■Graphics Kiyoko Takeda Chiaki Teramoto

Japanese: 成歩堂さんには、また お世話になってしまいました。
English: Mr. Wright, I am once again in your debt.

Japanese: おかげさまで《倉院の里・秘宝展》 は、大成功なんですよ！
English: Thanks to you, the Treasures of Kurain exhibit was a great success!

Japanese: １年ぶりに、アメリカから 狩魔検事さんもお見えになって。
English: I even got to see Ms. von Karma, who I hadn't seen for almost a year.

Japanese: ムチの使い方も教わったんです。 成歩堂さんに、お見せしないと‥‥
English: She taught me how to use a whip, and said that I must show you what I've learned...

Japanese: ■グラフィック 平松　昌子 西村　博司
English: ■Graphics Akiko Hiramatsu Hiroshi Nishimura

Japanese: ボクたち、会社を始めました。 ‥‥《怪人コンサルタント》。
English: Dessie and I started a company called, "Mask☆DeMasque Consulting".

Japanese: 街にはびこる怪人たちの大作戦を、 華麗にブッつぶすんだよ！
English: We're dedicated to stopping the evil plans of all the criminals in the city!

Japanese: 《やめてくださーーーい！》<Desirée> ‥‥が キメゼリフ。カッコイイんだから！
English: Our motto is, "Cut it out, pleeeeeeeease!" Pretty cute, huh!?

Japanese: まあ、その怪人たちに犯行計画を 売るのも、ボクたちなんですけど。
English: Well, we also sell plans to the criminals as a kind of side-business...

Japanese: ‥‥‥いいのかな、これって。 イチバン悪いの、ボクたちなんじゃ
English: ...I wonder if that's OK. Sometimes I think maybe we're the worst criminals

Japanese: ■メインプログラム 工藤　寛之
English: ■Main Programming Hiroyuki Kudou

Japanese: ‥‥トラさまを退治してくれた リュウさまに、お礼がしたくて‥‥
English: Mr. Wright... I want to thank you for... exterminating... Don Tigre...

Japanese: ５０００万円、お貸ししました。 ‥‥お茶のセットもいっしょに。
English: I returned the $500,000... along with a tea set.

Japanese: わたしが‥‥‥‥をこめて 調合した、特濃のお茶‥‥
English: A special thick tea... One I mixed with... my own two hands...

Japanese: 今ごろ‥‥アレを飲んで‥‥ 三日坊主‥‥ククッ‥‥
English: I bet he's drinking it now... Win Through Compromise... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: ■プログラム 藤見　信 小池　猛
English: ■Programming Makoto Fujimi Takeshi Koike

Japanese: どうせワシはジャマモノさ！ 煮ても焼いても食えぬナマモノさ！
English: I'm just old and in the way! A wrinkly, grumpy, clown- nosed waste of flesh!

Japanese: ‥‥と、思ってたら。
English: At least, that's what I thought...

Japanese: こないだ、マゴたちが お誕生日会を開いてくれましてな。
English: But my grandkids had a birthday party for me the other day.

Japanese: いやー、おはずかしい。 男・将兵。６９にして号泣ですわ。
English: Talk about embarrassing. 69 years old and I cried like a baby with a dirty diaper!

Japanese: ■音楽 岩垂　徳行
English: ■Music Noriyuki Iwadare

Japanese: 今日もここは、キナくさい ヒミツでいっぱいなんだぞー。
English: As usual, we have an abundance of work to do.

Japanese: 岡　高夫くんのかわりに、 新しいプログラマーが来たよー。
English: We've hired a new programmer to replace Glen Elg.

Japanese: みんな、おトモダチになろうねー。 ‥‥本塚　カズトモくん。
English: I do hope everyone will get along. His name is... Adam Mada.

Japanese: 名前を聞いた瞬間、おねえさんの 回路に電流が走ったんだねー。
English: As soon as I heard his name, I knew our brain circuits would align perfectly.

Japanese: ■サウンド 木村　明美
English: ■Sound Effects Akemi Kimura

Japanese: あらあらあらあら。 また取材かしら。
English: My, my, my. More reporters?

Japanese: あの事件以来、もうかって しょうがないのよねー、ホント。
English: Since the murder, we've made so much money, I hardly know what to do!

Japanese: やっぱり、オバサンのビジュアルが グーだから。‥‥特に、春先は。
English: I think the magazines like us because I provide such a nice visual. Especially in Spring.

Japanese: ゆっくり、あやめを待ちましょ。 わはは。はは。わはははは。
English: I can hardly wait for Iris to come back. Wa ha ha ho ho ho!

Japanese: ■制作協力 岡本　匡博 丸永　克己
English: ■Special Thanks Masahiro Okamoto Katsumi Marunaga

Japanese: ■制作協力 末包　久美子
English: ■Special Thanks Kumiko Suekane

Japanese: ‥‥リュウちゃん、本当に リッパになられましたね。
English: ...You've turned into such a respectable man, Feenie.

Japanese: 先日、みなさんで面会に いらしてくれたのですよ。
English: It was so sweet of you and everyone else to come and visit me here the other day.

Japanese: 私、うれしくて。リュウちゃんに うっとり、見とれていましたら‥‥
English: Of course I was happy that you constantly had your eyes on me, but...

Japanese: 小さな女の子が、リュウちゃんに 鼻血が出るまでビンタを‥‥。
English: ...I felt kind of bad when the little one slapped you so hard you got a nose bleed.

Japanese: ■声の出演■
English: ■Voice■

Japanese: ■成歩堂　龍一 巧　　舟
English: ■Phoenix Wright Ben "Gromin" Judd

Japanese: ■御剣　怜侍 岩元　辰郎
English: ■Miles Edgeworth Seon King

Japanese: ■綾里　千尋 河原　深雪
English: ■Mia Fey Christina Katano

Japanese: ■ゴドー 神谷　英樹
English: ■Godot James C. Wilson

Japanese: ■狩魔　冥 諏訪部　ゆかり
English: ■Franziska von Karma Janet Hsu

Japanese: ■亜内　武文 浜　　亘
English: ■Winston Payne David Crislip

Japanese: なんだい！　旅行から帰ってきた ってのに、お迎えもなしかい！
English: What's this!? I'm back from a long and tiring vacation and no one is here to greet me!?

Japanese: 相棒の若いコは、知らない間に やめちまってるし‥‥
English: I guess while I was gone, my little whippersnapper buddy quit and now I've got no one!

Japanese: だいたい、なんなのサ！ このイカれたケービ室は！
English: And what kind of lonely, crazy security room is this supposed to be anyway!?

Japanese: これじゃオバチャン、どこから 見ても宇宙人じゃないのヨ！
English: What with all the flashing lights and switches, I feel like some sort of space alien!

Japanese: おみやげに買ってきた、この大量の マカダミアナッツ、いったい、どう
English: And now what am I going to do with all these macadamia nuts I brought back for everyone!?

Japanese: すりゃいいんだい！　ミッちゃんの トコに持ってったって、どうせホレ
English: If I bring them over to Edgey- poo, I know exactly what he'd say the instant he saw them.

Japanese: 『そのようなアレは困る』みたいな コト言われてセツないッちゅう話サ
English: "I really can't accept these, I'm afraid," or something just as painful to this fragile

Japanese: どこかにオバチャンにお似合いの マメ好きな紳士はいないのかねェ
English: heart. Oh, when will I ever find a gentleman who will treat me like a refined lady?

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ■プロデュース 稲葉　敦志
English: ■Producer Atsushi Inaba

Japanese: オレも、やっと自分の やりたいコト、見つけてさァ！
English: I finally found something I love to do!

Japanese: 《メイちゃんのムチムチ大冒険》で 絵本界に革命を起こすんだ！
English: "Franzy's Whip Lash Splash" is gonna turn the art world on its head!

Japanese: ‥‥なんてったって、オレ。 ゲージュツカだし。
English: I should have realized it sooner. Self-centered, lazy, anti-social...

Japanese: 似顔絵だけは自信あるからさ。 もう、負け犬なんかじゃねえぜ！
English: I'm an artist. A really good portrait artist! I'm not a loser after all!

Japanese: ■製作総指揮 三上　真司
English: ■Executive Producer Shinji Mikami
